name: Black Forest Cheesecake 2
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Morello cherry syrup or sour cherry syrup
16 oz Cream cheese, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak cherries and kirsch in a small bowl for 6 hours.
2. Thoroughly drain the cherries in a strainer set over a medium bowl,
shaking occasionally, for at least two hours.
3. Reserve the liquid. Add enough Morello cherry syrup to the cherry
liquid to measure 1 cup. Pour into a heavy 8-inch skillet (reserve
remaining liquid for filling). Halve the cherries and add to the
skillet. Boil until the syrup is thickened and the mixture resembles
preserves, about 6 minutes.
4. Generously butter a 9-inch springform pan. Finely crush the cookies in
a processor, using on/off turns. Cut in the butter until the mixture
begins to gather together, using on/off turns. Press crumbs into the
bottom of the pan and up the sides to 3/4 inch from the top.
Refrigerate the crust for at least 30 minutes.
5. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees F. Heat 1 1/2 cups cream with chocolate
in a heavy medium saucepan over low heat until the chocolate melts,
stirring constantly. Cool for 10 minutes.
6. Beat the cream cheese with 3/4 cup sugar until smooth. Beat in the eggs
one at a time until just combined. Beat in the chocolate mixture, then
the remaining 10 tablespoons cherry liquid and vanilla. Pour into the crust.
7. Bake until the outer 2 inches of cake are firm but the center still moves
slightly, about 1 1/4 hours (top may crack). Cool completely on a rack.
8. Top the pan with paper towels and cover tightly with foil. Refrigerate for
1 to 2 days.
9. Remove the foil, paper towels, and pan sides from the cake. Spread the cherry
topping over the cake. Beat the remaining 1 cup cream with 2 tablespoons sugar
and kirsch to peaks. Spoon into the center of the cake. Top with chocolate curls if desired.
Let stand at room temperature for 15 minutes before serving.
